purpose: Anti-nonsense gate for road-mode shipment readiness. Determines whether a country-pair road corridor is liberalized, permit-required, restricted, ferry-dependent, or likely impractical.
design_principle: Code does deterministic lookups and math; LLM performs judgment over these gates and confidence caps.
known_limitations: No free global country-pair bilateral road-permit database was found; bilateral permit rows are framework/fallback records, not full legal clearance.
Live border closures, sanctions updates, strikes, ferry capacity, real-time border waits, security conditions and live tolls are out of scope for static v1.
UK operator-specific rules are verified for UK guidance context and must not be generalized to all operators.
Europe-North Africa logic is strong for Tunisia/Morocco examples in UK guidance, but exact bilateral requirements still need country-pair authority confirmation.
Intercontinental overland corridors are intentionally flagged as likely impractical unless a specialized carrier feasibility study is provided.
This bundle validates corridor viability only; it does not estimate route, price, distance, or live transit time.
